subject: [PATCH v7 2/2] mm: use mapping_max_folio_order() for force_thp_readahead order

reviews.author: Pedro Falcato
reviews.summary: Reviewer Pedro Falcato expressed a concern that the patch may introduce performance degradation, but overall approved it (LGTM) and requested further testing to confirm its impact.
reviews.raw_body:
Reviewed-by: Pedro Falcato <pfalcato@suse.de>

Overall LGTM. Lets see if performance doesn't degrade.

/me hides in cover

-- 
Pedro

2026-06-04.patch_summary: This patch modifies the force_thp_readahead path in do_sync_mmap_readahead() to use mapping_max_folio_order() instead of hardcoding HPAGE_PMD_ORDER. This allows VM_HUGEPAGE mappings on certain configurations, such as arm64 with a 64K base page size, to use forced readahead for large folios. The patch caps the maximum THP order at 2M, which is a common PMD-sized hugepage size and avoids memory pressure from very large forced readahead. This change improves performance by reducing TLB pressure on the readahead path.
